title: OSI Resolution Schema
description: Schema for defining conflict resolution rules when multiple mappings target the same dataset.

Resolution.description: Resolution rules for a single target dataset

name.description: Unique name for this resolution configuration

target.description: The target dataset these rules apply to

fields.description: Per-field resolution rules. Keys are target field names.

atomic_groups.description: Named atomic resolution groups. Fields in the same group resolve together: the source with the highest timestamp across any field in the group wins all grouped fields.

link_groups.description: Named link groups for entity linking. Records from different sources are linked only when ALL fields in the group match (tuple equality). Keys are group names.

FieldResolution.description: Resolution rules for a single target field

AtomicGroup.description: An atomic resolution group. All listed fields resolve from the same winning source — the source with the highest timestamp across any field in the group.

fields.description: Target field names that resolve together. All must use LAST_MODIFIED strategy.

LinkGroup.description: A link group for entity linking. Records from different sources are considered the same entity only when ALL listed fields match (tuple equality). Unlike single-field COLLECT with link, this requires the entire tuple to match, not just individual fields.

fields.description: Target field names that must ALL match for two records to be linked. Order does not matter.

oneOf.description: Pick the value from the highest-priority source (lowest number wins)
Pick the value from the most recently updated source
Collect all source values into an array. When link is true, values are also used for entity linking (transitive closure).

link.description: When true, records sharing this field's value are linked as the same entity. All values are retained for transitive closure.

oneOf.description: Resolve using a custom SQL expression operating in an aggregation context over all contributed values. The expression references the target field name directly as the column name.

expression.description: SQL aggregation expression. Use the target field name as the column name (e.g. MAX(email) for a field named 'email').
